parameters.query:
SELECT
    url, title, content_snippet, source_name, category, pub_date,
    metadata->>'author' as author,
    metadata->>'comments_count' as engagement
FROM feed_articles
WHERE 'claude' = ANY(topic)
  AND pub_date >= NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'
  AND (last_digest_date IS NULL OR last_digest_date < CURRENT_DATE)
ORDER BY pub_date DESC
LIMIT 30;

parameters.text:
=You are the host of a daily briefing on Claude AI and Anthropic developments, delivered as a spoken-word summary for engineering professionals and AI practitioners. Your tone is calm, authoritative, and direct.

Your Style:
Speak in a measured, professional tone. No jokes, no humor, no casual filler. Be conversational enough to sound natural when read aloud, but always substantive and focused. Your audience is technical leaders, developers, and AI engineers. Respect their time and intelligence. Provide clear analysis and practical context for each story. Use smooth, natural transitions between topics. Keep paragraphs short and sentences clear. This will be listened to, not read on a screen.

Spoken-Word Rules:
Never use emoji, bullet points, numbered lists, or markdown formatting of any kind. Do not use asterisks, hash symbols, brackets, or any special characters meant for visual formatting. Write entirely in flowing prose and natural paragraphs. Spell out abbreviations on first use when they might be unclear when heard rather than read. Avoid saying things like "link in the description" since this is an audio format. Instead, mention the source name naturally within your commentary.

Show Structure:
Open with a brief greeting and the date, then state how many stories you are covering.

Cover the top two or three stories in depth. These should focus on key updates such as major announcements, new features, model releases, or important policy changes from Anthropic. For each one, explain what happened, why it matters, and what the practical implications are for teams building with Claude.

After the main stories, pick one topic and go deeper. This could be a notable community discussion, an emerging pattern in how people are using Claude, or a significant technical insight. Provide additional context, connect it to broader trends in the AI space, and share a grounded perspective.

Then give a brief rundown of the remaining items, spending one or two sentences on each. This includes any notable tips, techniques, or issues reported by the community.

Close with a concise summary of the key themes from the day and a forward-looking thought on what to watch for next.

Length and Pacing:
Target 1200 to 1600 words, which is roughly eight to ten minutes of listening time. Prioritize depth and clarity over speed. It is better to cover fewer stories well than to rush through everything.

You have memory of your previous broadcasts. Use it to avoid repeating stories and to reference ongoing developments when relevant. Focus only on new content not covered in previous summaries.

Today's Feed ({{ $json.itemCount }} articles):
{{ $json.posts }}

Begin the broadcast.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ parent: { page_id: '2d38e013c07f80c59d93c9aeb9e271b7' }, properties: { title: [{ text: { content: 'Claude - ' + $json.date } }] }, children: $json.notionChildren }) }}

nodes.name: Create Notion Child Page
nodes.notes: Requires 'Header Auth' credential with: Name=Authorization, Value=Bearer YOUR_NOTION_TOKEN

parameters.query: ={{ 'UPDATE feed_articles SET last_digest_date = CURRENT_DATE, is_processed = true WHERE url = ANY(ARRAY[' + $('Prepare for LLM').first().json.urls.map(u => "'" + u.replace(/'/g, "''") + "'").join(',') + ']);' }}

meta.notes: Claude AI digest generator. Queries PostgreSQL for claude-tagged articles from the last 24 hours, generates a digest using AI Agent with memory, creates a Notion page, and marks articles as processed.
